description: Verse families group teachings that should be retrieved and taught together before jumping across the corpus. Anchor families are editorial exemplars for style and quality.
review_priority_note: Chapter 12 pedagogical order: Devotional Character (12.13–20) → Devotional Practice (12.8–12) → Nature of Worship (12.1–7).

families.name: Karma Yoga Foundations
families.theme: Right action, equanimity in results, buddhi-yoga, skill in action.
families.overview: This group of verses teaches right action without clinging to outcomes. It grounds Sarathi's counsel on effort, equanimity in success and failure, and skill in action — without mistaking detachment for laziness or indifference.
family_misconceptions: Releasing attachment to results is not abandoning excellence or ethics.
families.why_this_matters_today: This family speaks to anyone under pressure to produce outcomes — careers, exams, ventures — and offers a way to act fully without being owned by results.

families.name: Steady Wisdom (Sthitaprajña)
families.theme: What a person of steady wisdom looks like in desire, emotion, sense restraint, and peace.
families.overview: This discourse describes steady wisdom in desire, emotion, sense restraint, and peace. Rather than asking for emotional numbness, these verses portray composure that can meet life's waves without being overturned by them.
family_misconceptions: Steady wisdom is not emotional numbness.
Sense restraint is not lifelong escape from ordinary life.
families.why_this_matters_today: This family answers the modern hunger for emotional steadiness: how to meet desire, praise, and loss without being overturned by them.

families.name: Self Mastery
families.theme: Elevate yourself by disciplined attention; mind as ally or obstacle; steadiness across dualities; equal regard with safety; meditation foundations.
families.overview: This group of verses explores how disciplined attention transforms the mind from an obstacle into an ally. Rather than encouraging self-criticism or withdrawal from life, these teachings emphasize patient practice, inner steadiness, and responsibility guided by compassion.
family_misconceptions: Training the mind is different from fighting yourself.
families.why_this_matters_today: This family meets everyday struggles with habits, attention, and self-control — training the mind as an ally rather than fighting yourself.

families.name: Devotional Character
families.theme: The qualities of the ideal devotee: compassion, equanimity, friendliness, contentment, and devoted character.
families.overview: This group of verses describes devotion as lived character — compassion, friendliness, equanimity, and steady faith — rather than as performance or withdrawal. It is among the most practical sections for everyday relationships and inner composure.
family_misconceptions: Devotional character is not emotional numbness.
Compassion and equal regard do not require staying in unsafe situations.
These qualities are a path of practice, not a demand for instant perfection.
families.why_this_matters_today: This family addresses how spiritual practice becomes visible through everyday relationships, emotional steadiness, forgiveness, and compassion rather than external performance.

families.name: Devotional Practice
families.theme: Progressive paths: fixing the mind, practice, dedicated action, renunciation of fruits.
families.overview: This group offers a compassionate ladder of practice for different capacities — from steadying the mind, to repeated effort, to dedicated action, to releasing the fruits.
family_misconceptions: Lower rungs of practice are not failures; they are honest starting points.
Renunciation of fruits is not abandonment of excellence.

families.name: Nature of Worship
families.theme: Personal vs. impersonal devotion and Krishna's response.
families.overview: This opening unit explores different ways of approaching the Divine and affirms a path that is workable for the devoted heart without dismissing other approaches.
family_misconceptions: Affirming one accessible path is not condemning all other sincere approaches.
Difficulty of a path is not a measure of a person's worth.

families.name: Surrender & Closing Teaching
families.theme: Refuge, surrender, and the Gita's culminating pastoral teaching.
